title: Swing Vote

jokes.text: First of all, I am rage glowing.
jokes.setup: Character corrects Ron's description of 'rage sweat'
jokes.punchline: Substitutes 'glowing' for 'sweating' as a more positive spin
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie optimism - turning something negative into positive terminology. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: What did the P.P.P.P.P. ever do to you?
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions cutting funding for Pawnee Palms Public Putt-Putt
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous acronym with five P's in a row
jokes.explanation: Amusing alliterative absurdity that fits Leslie's bureaucratic enthusiasm for naming things.

jokes.text: Schools, police, mini-golf, merry-go-rounds, parades, gazebo repair, roads and bridges, whatever. Pretty gardens, hummingbird feeders.
jokes.setup: Leslie lists things government shouldn't cut
jokes.punchline: Mix of essential services with frivolous items treated equally, ending with casual 'whatever'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character work - her inability to prioritize government services, treating gazebo repair as essential as police.

jokes.text: I believe in cutting useless government projects. I also believe in cutting useful projects, future projects, and past projects. The Hoover Dam is a travesty.
jokes.setup: Ron explains his anti-government philosophy
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable to completely absurd - even past projects and iconic infrastructure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that perfectly captures Ron's extreme libertarian views taken to their logical absurd conclusion.

jokes.text: Do you expect me to make a chair out of transistor chips and small wires?
jokes.setup: Leslie tells Ron to go make furniture instead of cutting budgets
jokes.punchline: Ron takes her electronics reference literally with confused practicality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ron's literal mindedness and woodworking expertise clash with modern technology.

jokes.text: And it'll produce double the amount of electricity. - How? - I'll figure it out. It already has windmills. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Leslie claims the putt-putt will produce more electricity than Hoover Dam
jokes.punchline: Admits she has no plan but mentions decorative windmills as if they're relevant
jokes.explanation: Leslie's optimistic delusion and hand-wavy problem-solving perfectly in character.

jokes.text: - Yeah, I know. - I feel very ill.
jokes.setup: Leslie compliments how handsome both councilmen look
jokes.punchline: Jamm accepts compliment while Milton feels sick from it
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast between characters' reactions to flattery.

jokes.text: Well, the last time I was there, I saw kids of all races getting along and playing. It was terrible.
jokes.setup: Milton appears to give positive reason for supporting mini-golf
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually racist and this is why he opposes it
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection that efficiently establishes character as cartoonishly awful. Dark but well-crafted.

jokes.text: Well, on the one hand, I love cutting government programs. On the other hand, mini-golf rules. And on the third hand, I hate both you and Ron Swanson.
jokes.setup: Jamm weighs his decision factors
jokes.punchline: Adds impossible third hand and admits to petty personal hatred
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd anatomy and honest pettiness that defines Jamm's character.

jokes.text: I'm kind of a badass wild card.
jokes.setup: Jamm explains his unpredictable voting
jokes.punchline: Describes himself as 'badass wild card' which is pathetic rather than cool
jokes.explanation: Jamm's delusional self-image perfectly captured in cringey self-description.

jokes.text: Hey, what are you doing tonight? More like who am I doing? No one. I'm free. What's up?
jokes.setup: Jamm tries to sound sexually successful
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's doing no one and is available
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe moment - character's attempt at coolness immediately undercut by reality.

jokes.text: I am? You drove here. Nah, it's too late, bro. I already ordered the water. I don't know what you want me to do.
jokes.setup: Ben realizes Andy designated him as driver despite Andy driving
jokes.punchline: Andy's illogical commitment to the water order
jokes.explanation: Andy's childlike logic and inability to correct course once committed to something.

jokes.text: And somehow I just ended up becoming the designated driver. And paying for everyone. And I didn't get to choose the bar. I should be more assertive.
jokes.setup: Ben lists multiple ways he's been taken advantage of
jokes.punchline: Mild conclusion that he should be 'more assertive' after being completely steamrolled
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character moment - his passive nature and understated response to being walked all over.

jokes.text: Kinda sounds like one of your songs, babe.
jokes.setup: Andy compliments the band's sound
jokes.punchline: April suggests it sounds like Andy's notoriously bad music
jokes.explanation: Classic April deadpan insult disguised as casual observation.

jokes.text: I didn't recognize us without me because I'm the only one that matters.
jokes.setup: Andy realizes it's his band playing without him
jokes.punchline: Narcissistic explanation for not recognizing his own band
jokes.explanation: Andy's oblivious self-importance delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: We are Rat Mouse.
jokes.setup: Band needs new name without Andy
jokes.punchline: Simply reverses 'Mouse Rat' with no creativity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective absurdist humor - the laziest possible band name change.

jokes.text: Hey, baby, can I borrow your car for a month? I already lent you my car. Do you not know where my car is? Ugh, I'm so sick of the third degree. It's like dating a cop.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa asks to borrow car for unreasonably long time
jokes.punchline: Reveals she already has his car and lost it, then complains about being questioned
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of entitled behavior and victim complex. Establishes Mona-Lisa's toxicity efficiently.

jokes.text: I'm gassy. Let's make out.
jokes.setup: Normal conversation moment
jokes.punchline: Random disgusting non-sequitur followed by romantic suggestion
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that establishes Mona-Lisa as completely inappropriate and gross.

jokes.text: Dating Mona-Lisa is awesome. Except that I live in constant fear for my life.
jokes.setup: Tom claims dating Mona-Lisa is awesome
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts with admission of constant fear
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup with Tom's delusional self-assessment undercut by reality.

jokes.text: Calm down, Ann. You're proving my point.
jokes.setup: Tom asks Ann to help with his 'crazy' girlfriend, Ann objects to being called crazy
jokes.punchline: Tom uses her reasonable objection as evidence of craziness
jokes.explanation: Clever circular logic that references Tom's pattern while being immediately self-defeating.

jokes.text: She once jumped out of a moving car to buy a Nicki Minaj poster.
jokes.setup: Tom needs to prove Mona-Lisa is actually crazy
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd example that's both dangerous and trivial
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of absurd behavior - specific, dangerous, and for something completely trivial.

jokes.text: Yeah, dead ringer for Courtney Thorne-Smith. She works at the gas station.
jokes.setup: Context suggests he might be describing someone attractive
jokes.punchline: Comparing gas station worker to 90s TV actress in completely random context
jokes.explanation: Weird random celebrity reference that's more confusing than funny. Feels like a throwaway line.

jokes.text: I can be a sort of... Conversational lubricant.
jokes.setup: Chris offers to keep things light and upbeat
jokes.punchline: Chooses grossly inappropriate metaphor for social facilitation
jokes.explanation: Chris's oblivious inappropriate phrasing perfectly in character. Memorable cringe moment.

jokes.text: Yeah, there's probably a less gross way to say it, but sure.
jokes.setup: Chris's 'conversational lubricant' comment
jokes.punchline: Leslie's understated acknowledgment that it was disgusting but pragmatically accepts help
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that acknowledges the grossness while moving forward pragmatically.

jokes.text: I consider myself a caddy to everyone in my life. Yeah, good one! Nice shot! Great sweeping. Way to be, duck.
jokes.setup: Chris explains his supportive philosophy
jokes.punchline: Examples get increasingly random, ending with encouraging a duck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - his excessive positivity applied to increasingly absurd situations.

jokes.text: Some fat Hawaiian guy left it in my waiting room. When he came back, I was all like, 'Oh, uh, yeah, yeah, no, I haven't seen it. I don't know.' Classic.
jokes.setup: Jamm compliments on his putter
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to stealing it with racist description and thinks it's funny
jokes.explanation: Effectively establishes Jamm as terrible person through casual admission of theft with racist overtones.

jokes.text: You know I have irritable bowel syndrome, you racist.
jokes.setup: Leslie offers hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Jamm refuses for medical reasons but calls it racist
jokes.explanation: Jamm's tendency to make everything about persecution even when it's clearly not.

jokes.text: I was just over at the bar, enjoying a wonderful night with my co-workers who all talked about how awesome I was, and I look up, and what do I see?
jokes.setup: Andy dramatically confronts his band
jokes.punchline: Claims co-workers were praising him (obviously false) with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Andy's delusional self-perception delivered with wounded drama.

jokes.text: First of all, your nachos are terrible. No one's had the guts to tell you. Why don't you melt the cheese?
jokes.setup: Band member confronts Andy about his contributions
jokes.punchline: Specific criticism about not melting cheese - basic cooking failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Andy's incompetence - can't even manage basic nacho preparation.

jokes.text: Well, I dropped my cell phone in a bowl of cereal last week, you idiot.
jokes.setup: Andy explains why band couldn't reach him
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous accident that's perfectly in character
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy mishap - absurd but believable given his character.

jokes.text: Maybe you should change your name to Burly and The Backstabbers. That's a pretty good name, actually. Yeah, he's really good at coming up with names.
jokes.setup: Andy angrily suggests insulting band name
jokes.punchline: Band members genuinely like it and compliment his naming skills
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - Andy's angry insult becomes genuine compliment, undermining his dramatic moment.

jokes.text: Get me a Budweiser Black Crown. You guys got Black Crown here? If not, you should.
jokes.setup: Andy orders beer dramatically
jokes.punchline: Specific brand request followed by business advice to random bar
jokes.explanation: Andy's misplaced confidence extending to bar management advice.

jokes.text: You guys sound like school. I have two important pieces of caddy information. Councilman Jamm leads by eight strokes, and you are both about to set a course record. For friendship.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ron argue about taxpayer costs while playing mini-golf
jokes.punchline: Jamm dismisses them like a bored kid, Chris delivers optimistic spin on their arguing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Jamm's childish dismissal and Chris's relentless positivity about conflict.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Ann Perkins. - I used to date Tom. - Oh, okay. We can do this, but I will bite you.
jokes.setup: Ann introduces herself politely to Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa immediately threatens violence
jokes.explanation: Immediate escalation from normal introduction to threat of biting establishes Mona-Lisa's insanity.

jokes.text: Do you know his ATM pin? 'Cause I cannot crack that little bitch's code.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa asks Ann about Tom
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to trying to steal from him with insulting language
jokes.explanation: Perfectly establishes Mona-Lisa as criminal and abusive while treating it casually.

jokes.text: Diddy's on Instagram? How did I not know that Diddy was on Instagram, you jagweeds?
jokes.setup: Ann mentions Tom's Diddy Instagram coasters
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa gets distracted by celebrity social media and yells at imaginary people
jokes.explanation: Mona-Lisa's scattered attention span and random hostility toward 'jagweeds.'

jokes.text: I love Andy, but to be perfectly honest, Mouse Rat's music is not my thing. I really only listen to, like, German Death Reggae and Halloween sound effects records from the 1950s. And Bette Midler. Obviously.
jokes.setup: April discusses Andy's music
jokes.punchline: Lists incredibly specific obscure genres then adds mainstream Bette Midler as obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - specific weird interests punctuated by unexpected mainstream addition treated as obvious.

jokes.text: What do you need a computer fan for? Sometimes when you use a computer, it gets hot, and the fan cools it down.
jokes.setup: Andy lists songwriting supplies including computer fan
jokes.punchline: Explains computer fans with childlike obviousness when questioned
jokes.explanation: Andy's literal-minded explanation that completely misses why it's weird to need for songwriting.

jokes.text: Tommy owns his own business. Yeah, but he had to borrow a lot of money, and when you're not around, Tom drinks tap water.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa defends Tom's financial status
jokes.punchline: Ann reveals his secret poverty through specific detail about tap water
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that exposes Tom's financial situation through something he'd consider humiliating.

jokes.text: Mm, yeah, 'cause I don't eff with poorsies.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa learns Tom is broke
jokes.punchline: Casual classist dismissal with juvenile euphemism
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Mona-Lisa moment - terrible values expressed through immature language.

jokes.text: and I don't even usually like Puerto Rican chicks. I'm not--I mean, it shouldn't matter, but I'm--
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa 'compliments' Ann while breaking up with Tom
jokes.punchline: Racist assumption about Ann's ethnicity that Ann tries to correct
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing Mona-Lisa's casual racism and Ann's awkward position.

jokes.text: My name is Andy Dwyer. You probably know me as the artist formerly known as the lead singer of Mouse Rat. Well, after tonight, you'll only know me as the former artist formerly known as the lead singer of Mouse Rat.
jokes.setup: Andy introduces his farewell performance
jokes.punchline: Confusing nested 'formerly known as' construction that gets tangled
jokes.explanation: Andy's attempt at dramatic formality gets linguistically confused in perfectly characteristic way.

jokes.text: ♪ He made incredible nachos / That everybody claimed they loved / And then the band mates dicked him over / With a massive rock and roll shove ♪
jokes.setup: Andy's swan song about his band experience
jokes.punchline: References his terrible nachos that he thinks were incredible
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Andy's complete lack of self-awareness about his cooking.

jokes.text: Tom. What happened to you? Mona-Lisa happened to me. She covered me in glitter and Anna Nicole body spray.
jokes.setup: Tom appears disheveled
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd details of his assault by Mona-Lisa
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy through specific absurd details that suggest chaotic assault.

jokes.text: Why do they still even make that? They don't--she's had the same bottle for years. She just mixes in water and baby oil.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Anna Nicole body spray
jokes.punchline: Reveals Mona-Lisa has been diluting old perfume with gross additives
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that adds disgusting details about Mona-Lisa's hygiene habits.

jokes.text: Why is there a gorilla guarding this gingerbread house?
jokes.setup: Playing mini-golf hole with themed obstacles
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal-minded question about nonsensical mini-golf design
jokes.explanation: Ron's practical mindset confronting the illogical whimsy of mini-golf theming.

jokes.text: Uh, bean sprouts. Tofu. Ralph Nader. Ayn Rand is a terrible writer. Don't mess up, don't mess up, don't mess up.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to distract Ron during crucial putt
jokes.punchline: Lists things she thinks will annoy Ron, including attack on his philosophical hero
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - knows exactly what will bother Ron and escalates to his literary heroes.

jokes.text: What the mother-effing, c-ing ess-ing, effing k-ing eff is going on right now?
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa discovers Tom and Ann kissing
jokes.punchline: Elaborate euphemistic swearing that's more complex than just swearing
jokes.explanation: Mona-Lisa's over-the-top censored swearing that's more effort than regular profanity.

jokes.text: You guys want a threesome. What? No. We do not wanna have a threesome with you. Well, let's not rush to judgment. I mean, I say we hear her out.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa misinterprets Tom and Ann's fake relationship
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately considers the offer despite trying to get rid of her
jokes.explanation: Tom's weakness and poor priorities immediately undermining the plan.

jokes.text: I've been single for an hour now, and it's the tits!
jokes.setup: Tom celebrates being free from Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Extremely brief period of freedom treated as major achievement
jokes.explanation: Tom's pathetic celebration of minimal independence perfectly captures his character.

jokes.text: This is just how I dress now. Funny goofball music Andy... Is gone. You're left with the professional remains.
jokes.setup: Andy appears in business attire
jokes.punchline: Dramatic announcement of personality change with overwrought language
jokes.explanation: Andy's dramatic self-reinvention with theatrical language that's immediately undercut.

jokes.text: Give me more time to play video games.
jokes.setup: Andy explains benefits of focusing on business
jokes.punchline: Real reason is more time for childish pursuits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - adult decision motivated by childish priorities.

jokes.text: I would pay any amount of money to properly shame you.
jokes.setup: Ron asks how much Leslie paid for the gorilla
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate commitment to making a point regardless of cost
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her over-commitment to making symbolic points.

jokes.text: I will call him '$9,000 of taxpayer money the gorilla.' That is a terrible-- his name is Mr. Fuzzyface!
jokes.setup: Ron names the gorilla after the cost Leslie mentioned
jokes.punchline: Leslie's indignant correction with adorable name
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Ron's fiscal focus vs Leslie's emotional attachment and cute naming.

jokes.text: That is a terrible-- his name is Mr. Fuzzyface!
jokes.setup: Ron's bureaucratic name for the gorilla
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate emotional attachment and much cuter name
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Leslie's emotional approach versus Ron's practical one. The name 'Mr. Fuzzyface' is perfectly Leslie.

jokes.text: Well, Mona-Lisa and the random girl she grabbed after you left got into a fistfight in the parking lot. Eventually, Mona-Lisa threw a cinderblock through the girl's windshield. She dives in, steals her purse, doesn't go for the money, steals all her birth control pills, and says, 'Bitch, you're gonna get pregnant.' She then pretends those are ecstasy and sells it to a bunch of college kids that are drinking Goldschläger at a gas station.
jokes.setup: Ann asks about Tom's night with Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Increasingly chaotic and specific criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of chaos with specific absurd details. Each crime gets more random and awful. Near-legendary Parks and Rec moment.

jokes.text: She then pretends those are ecstasy and sells it to a bunch of college kids that are drinking Goldschläger at a gas station.
jokes.setup: Tom describing Mona-Lisa's crime spree
jokes.punchline: The escalation to drug fraud and the specific detail about Goldschläger at a gas station
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with vivid, specific details that paint a perfect picture of chaos.

jokes.text: My word is garbage. Everyone knows that.
jokes.setup: Leslie points out Jamm gave his word to Ron
jokes.punchline: Jamm casually admits his complete unreliability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm moment - complete lack of shame about being untrustworthy.

jokes.text: I have six things to say to you. One: You drive me nuts with your rigid code of honor. Two: Congratulations, putt-putt has been defunded. And only because Jamm was going to double-cross you, and I made him stick to his word. Three: I am furious that putt-putt has been defunded. Four: I am sorry that I said the gorilla's blood was on your hands. 'Twas Leslie killed the beast. Five: Putt-putt is for children, and they are the future, and I have already written a ballot measure that will save it, and it will pass. And six: Your rigid code of honor, which drives me nuts, makes you a wonderful human being, and I am proud to call you my friend, and don't ever change.
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to explain complex situation to Ron
jokes.punchline: Methodical numbered list that contradicts itself and ends with emotional declaration
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - over-organized approach to emotional complexity with perfect character contradictions.

jokes.text: And then you either realize that I'm right or you get hungry and go home and I win.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes ideal political discourse with Ron
jokes.punchline: Admits she usually wins through Ron's basic needs, not persuasion
jokes.explanation: Perfect insight into Leslie and Ron's relationship dynamic with Leslie's competitive nature.

jokes.text: Name one other band that's done that. The Beatles.
jokes.setup: Andy boasts about having two lead vocalists
jokes.punchline: April immediately names the most famous example
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and immediate deflation. April's deadpan delivery and Andy's obliviousness.

tmdb_overview: Leslie deems Ron's budget-cut proposals unacceptable. Andy and Ben celebrate a big donation made to their charity. Ann tries to solve Tom's girlfriend troubles.
insight: Divisive election setup underdelivers: 73 score despite character comedy strengths.
